name: Passover Blintzes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a food processor bowl and process until smooth, about 15 seconds.
2. Use a crepe pan or teflon skillet. Grease lightly for the first blintz.
3. Pour about 3 tablespoons batter (just enough to cover the bottom of the pan) into the skillet and cook about 45 seconds on one side only, until the top surface is dry.
4. Flip for 5 seconds on the second side. Turn out onto a tea towel.
5. Repeat with remaining batter. (May be prepared in advance up to this point and refrigerated or frozen until needed.)
6. Place about 3 tablespoons desired filling on blintz and roll up, turning in ends.
7. Brown in hot butter or oil on all sides, until golden.
8. (For a large quantity, you may bake blintzes. Heat about 1/2 cup butter or oil at 400°F in a 9" x 13-inch casserole. Add blintzes. Bake for 10 minutes. Turn over and bake for another 10 minutes, or until golden.)
Yield: About 16 blintzes. May be frozen.
I usually make potato and onion blintzes at Pesach using an electric crepe pan which does not need much fat to prevent sticking. A mixer or egg beater can be used instead of a food processor. Carole Walberg
